surface.name: Paddle.js overlay checkout quickstart (sandbox test payment)
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Take a test payment. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Paddle setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The overlay displays the successful checkout state after valid sandbox payment details are submitted.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Take a test payment.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Paddle.js overlay checkout quickstart (sandbox test payment) surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A web page capable of loading Paddle.js.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Paddle Sandbox.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create a product and price, then create or copy a sandbox client-side token.
primary_path.output: A sandbox price ID and client-side token are available.
primary_path.success_signal: A sandbox price ID and client-side token are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Load Paddle.js on a page, initialize it for sandbox with the client-side token, and configure an overlay checkout for the price.
primary_path.output: The page can open a Paddle overlay for the configured item.
primary_path.success_signal: The page can open a Paddle overlay for the configured item.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Open the overlay and submit the documented sandbox test payment details.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Confirm the checkout success state in the overlay.
primary_path.output: The test payment completion state is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The test payment completion state is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: The client-side token and price must belong to the sandbox environment.
Paddle.js must be loaded and initialized on a browser page in an allowed environment.

sources.title: Take a test payment
evidence_supported: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.
The overlay displays the successful checkout state after valid sandbox payment details are submitted.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Take a test payment path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
